question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: getFrequency()] Can an unprivileged external user submit transactions that cause repeated hash-chain verification failures, forcing the Downloader to re-enter downloadNextBatch() at the full fixedDelayString cadence without any back-off, sustaining CPU and network I/O above 30% baseline for the duration of the attack?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: isEnabled()] Can an unprivileged external user craft stream files whose consensus timestamp is just before endDate so that setEnabled(false) is never triggered, keeping the downloader permanently active and consuming maximum polling resources without any operator intervention?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: isPersistBytes()] Can an unprivileged external user submit transactions that produce stream files near the maxSize limit (50 MiB) so that when isPersistBytes() returns true the importer writes the full byte payload to the database on every polling cycle, causing sustained database write amplification exceeding 30% of normal I/O?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: isWriteFiles()] Can an unprivileged external user submit transactions that produce near-maxSize stream files so that when isWriteFiles() returns true, Utility.archiveFile() is called with 50 MiB payloads on every verified file, causing sustained disk I/O amplification that raises node resource consumption by more than 30%?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: isWriteSignatures()] Can an unprivileged external user submit transactions that produce a large number of consensus nodes' signature files so that when isWriteSignatures() returns true, Utility.archiveFile() is called for every signature in the collection on each verified batch, multiplying disk write operations and raising I/O resource consumption above 30%?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: getCommon()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that getCommon().getTimeout() is 30 seconds and that Flux uses .timeout(downloaderProperties.getCommon().getTimeout()) to hold all parallel download threads open for the full timeout window by causing the cloud provider to respond slowly, exhausting the Schedulers.parallel() thread pool and raising CPU/thread resource consumption above 30%?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: getFrequency()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that RecordFileDownloader.shouldDownload() always returns true (unconditionally) combined with the 500ms fixedDelayString frequency to ensure the downloader never skips a cycle, sustaining maximum polling frequency and raising CPU consumption above 30% compared to a baseline where some cycles are skipped?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: getStreamType()] Can an unprivileged external user submit transactions that produce both RECORD and BALANCE stream types simultaneously, causing both RecordFileDownloader and AccountBalancesDownloader to run concurrent download cycles, doubling the parallel Flux.fromIterable(nodes).flatMap() fan-out and raising aggregate CPU and network consumption above 30%?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: isEnabled()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the condition in downloadNextBatch() where sigFilesMap.isEmpty() on a DEMO network triggers setEnabled(false), and on non-DEMO networks no such guard exists, so the downloader continues consuming resources indefinitely even when no new files are available, sustaining unnecessary polling overhead above 30%?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: getCommon()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the batchSize=25 default in CommonDownloaderProperties so that by submitting transactions that produce exactly 25 signature files per node per cycle, the downloader always processes a full batch, maximizing per-cycle signature parsing and verification CPU work above 30% of a lighter-load baseline?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: getCommon()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the downloadRatio=1.0 default in CommonDownloaderProperties so that partialCollection() returns all consensus nodes, causing Flux.fromIterable(nodes).flatMap() to fan out to every node simultaneously, maximizing parallel HTTP connections and raising network/CPU resource consumption above 30%?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: isPersistBytes()] Can an unprivileged external user submit transactions that produce stream files with maximum-size embedded byte payloads so that when isPersistBytes() returns true, the in-memory streamFile.getBytes() is retained through the entire processing pipeline, causing heap memory pressure that triggers frequent GC cycles and raises JVM CPU consumption above 30%?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: isWriteFiles()] Can an unprivileged external user submit transactions that produce stream files with deeply nested directory paths in archiveDestinationFolder so that when isWriteFiles() returns true, Utility.archiveFile() must create many nested directories on each invocation, causing filesystem metadata operations to spike and raising I/O resource consumption above 30%?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: getFrequency()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that getFrequency() is used as fixedDelay (not fixedRate), meaning the next cycle starts immediately after the previous one completes, so by crafting transactions that cause each download cycle to complete quickly (e.g., empty files), the effective polling rate becomes much higher than the configured 500ms, raising CPU consumption above 30%?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: getCommon()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the maxSize=50 MiB limit in CommonDownloaderProperties by submitting transactions that produce stream files just under the limit, causing the importer to download, parse, and verify 50 MiB payloads on every cycle, raising memory allocation and CPU parsing work above 30% of a normal-sized-file baseline?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: isWriteSignatures()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that when isWriteSignatures() is true, signatures.forEach(s -> Utility.archiveFile(...)) iterates over all signatures in the collection (one per consensus node), so by ensuring all nodes produce signatures for every file, the attacker maximizes per-file disk write operations and raises I/O resource consumption above 30%?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: getCommon()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the pathRefreshInterval=10s in CommonDownloaderProperties by crafting transactions that cause path type detection (AUTO mode) to re-evaluate on every refresh, triggering repeated cloud bucket listing operations and raising network I/O resource consumption above 30%?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: isEnabled()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that setEnabled(false) is called inside verifySignatures() when dataFilename.getInstant().isAfter(endDate), and that this check happens after full file download and parsing, meaning the attacker can force a full 50 MiB download-and-parse cycle on the final file before the downloader disables itself, causing a one-time resource spike above 30%?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: getCommon()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the consensusRatio=0.333 default so that by submitting transactions that produce exactly the minimum number of matching signatures needed for consensus, the nodeSignatureVerifier.verify() must evaluate all available signatures before reaching the threshold, maximizing per-cycle cryptographic verification CPU work above 30%?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: getStreamType()] Can an unprivileged external user submit transactions that produce RECORD stream files with many sidecar references, causing RecordFileDownloader.downloadSidecars() to issue additional cloud storage GET requests for each sidecar file on top of the main record file download, multiplying network I/O and raising resource consumption above 30%?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: getFrequency()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the Scheduled annotation uses fixedDelayString with getFrequency().toMillis() evaluated at startup, so if the frequency is set to a very small value (e.g., 1ms minimum), the downloader polls at maximum rate, and the attacker can sustain this by ensuring there are always new files available, raising CPU consumption above 30%?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: isEnabled()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that AccountBalancesDownloader.shouldDownload() returns true even when enabled=false if no account balance file exists in the repository, causing the downloader to continue polling indefinitely and consuming resources above 30% of a properly-disabled baseline?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: getCommon()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the sources list in CommonDownloaderProperties where multiple sources are configured, causing the downloader to attempt downloads from all sources sequentially on failure, multiplying the number of cloud storage requests per cycle and raising network I/O resource consumption above 30%?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: isPersistBytes()] Can an unprivileged external user submit transactions that produce stream files with maximum byte payloads so that when isPersistBytes() is true, the streamFile.getBytes() array is stored in the lastStreamFile AtomicReference copy (via streamFile.copy().clear()), causing long-lived heap retention and raising JVM memory pressure above 30% of normal?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: getCommon()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the nodeSignatureStatusMetricMap ConcurrentHashMap in Downloader, which grows unboundedly as new nodeId/streamType/status combinations are encountered, by submitting transactions that cause many distinct signature status transitions across many nodes, causing unbounded metric counter registration and raising memory consumption above 30%?
